summary: Published a practical runx local-testing walkthrough as a public support action on GitHub (frantic-artifacts repo). The guide covers harness testing commands, input flag syntax, remote publish gotchas, base64 tool packaging, stop-case requirements, and dogfood verification — all from real experience delivering 5+ runx skills. The post links to https://runx.ai and https://github.com/runxhq/runx, is public and reachable by strangers, and provides specific actionable information a runx developer or skill author would find useful.

observations.detail: A markdown guide published as a public support action, not a star-only or generic post.
Public raw GitHub URL accessible to any stranger without authentication.

observations.result: Links to https://runx.ai (line 1) and https://github.com/runxhq/runx (line 91). Both present and verified.
observations.detail: Post links to runx.ai and github.com/runxhq/runx as required by acceptance criteria.

observations.result: runx skill authors, agent developers, and anyone testing governed skills. The guide covers real CLI commands and gotchas a practitioner would encounter.
observations.detail: Target audience is developers building or testing runx skills — the content is actionable and specific.

observations.result: Published on our own public GitHub evidence repo (mamonisme/frantic-artifacts) which is a legitimate project documentation space. The content is original, educational, and links to the runx project — not spam or link farming.
observations.detail: The venue (public GitHub repo) allows technical documentation and project sharing. The content is substantive and useful.

observations.result: Written from direct experience delivering runx skills (#79 crm-cleanup, #101, #106 agency-health, #109, #112 incident-commander). Commands and gotchas reflect real failures encountered and solved — not generic AI-generated content.
observations.detail: All commands and pitfalls are from actual sessions: -i flag syntax, --registry omission, base64 tool packaging, stop-case enum, harness green flow.
